{{nihongo|'''Iwami no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|石見国造|''iwami no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''iwami kokuzō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled [[#Territory|Iwami Province]].

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry ===
* According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]], '''Ōyako-no-Mikoto'''—son of Kagesanako-no-Mikoto (son of {{ill|Kitone-no-Mikoto|ja|鬼刀禰命}}), who shared the same ancestor as the {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀伊国造}}—was appointed kuni no miyatsuko.
* Ōyako-no-Mikoto is said to have lived in {{ill|Nima District|ja|邇摩郡}}.<ref>Amatsu Wataru (ed.), ''Iwami-shi'', Hakutoen, Taishō 14 (1925), [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1020958/106 p. 194].</ref>

=== Lineage ===
Unclear. Although they shared the same ancestral lineage as the {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}}, no genealogy of the kuni no miyatsuko lineage survives.

== Base ==
Unclear.

=== Territory ===
The kuni no miyatsuko's territory was the area known at the time as '''Iwami Province''', corresponding to the later {{ill|ritsuryō province|ja|律令国}} of '''[[Iwami Province]]'''.

== Tradition ==
At {{ill|Mononobe Shrine (Ōda)|ja|物部神社 (大田市)|lt=Mononobe Shrine}} in [[Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]], there is a tradition that Mononobe-no-Takeko-no-Muraji, ancestor of the priestly Kaneko clan, served as the Iwami no Kuni no Miyatsuko.<ref>[https://www.mononobe-jinja.jp/ Mononobe Shrine Official Website].</ref>

== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}

== See also ==
* {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:いわみのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:石見国]]
[[Category:島根県の歴史]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:石見国造]]
